meta.title: Retire @ Masterful 1 — Training Behaviour (Initial)
meta.meta_line: 697 users · Remarkable 3 → Masterful 1 · ±30d around rank-up · Jun 1–19, 2026 cohort
meta.filters: REPUTATION_LEAGUE 15→16; PLAYER_TRAINING_COST TP sink; levelUps from extra_data; batsman/bowler via char classification
meta.thesis: Rank-up exposure to Retire does not expand unique batsmen trained — users train hard on a similar roster size; next step is usage-based (actual Retire actions), not exposure-only.
meta.description: Initial Before/After training read around Masterful 1 unlock of Retire a Player — breadth (unique players/batsmen) vs intensity (level-ups).

thesis.headline: Retire unlock at Masterful 1 does not broaden batting training — depth up, roster breadth flat.
thesis.recommendation: Do not treat exposure-only Before/After as proof Retire expands batting investment. Next: split users who actually use Retire vs non-users and re-check unique batsmen trained.
caveats: This is feature exposure by rank-up, not confirmed Retire usage.
Before/After windows are ±30 days around first Masterful 1 — not causal isolation of Retire alone.
Training = sum of levelUps on PLAYER_TRAINING_COST TP sinks.

next_steps: Tag actual Retire usage (retire action / event) in the post-Masterful 1 window — not just rank exposure.
Compare Retire users vs non-users on unique batsmen trained and batsman level-ups (Before vs After).
Optional: lineup / finisher exposure after Retire (does retiring weak bats improve finisher usage?).
Optional: power / rarity mix of trained batsmen — finishers vs openers — if Retire is reallocating investment.

method.cohort: First REPUTATION_LEAGUE transition 15→16 (Remarkable 3 → Masterful 1) between Jun 1–19, 2026 · N=697
method.window: Per-user ±30 days around rank_up_date (Before = D-30…D-1; After = D0…D+30)
method.training: hws_logs.ucl · SOFT_CURRENCY catalogue 5 · PLAYER_TRAINING_COST · quantity < 0 · level_ups = extra_data.levelUps
method.primary_metric: Unique players / unique batsmen trained (breadth) — Retire hypothesis; level-ups as intensity
